title: Initiative Work Contracts — hypothesis-driven completion with focus-lock
perceive: Read WAI-Spoke/initiatives/index.json — verify all initiatives have the required contract fields: lifecycle_state, focus_lock, hypothesis, success_criteria, completion_gate. Check tools/spoke_expediter.py for load_focus_lock_ids() and 3x ROI multiplier logic. Check if impl-initiative-schema-contract-fields-v1 exists (may be missing — schema was applied via inline session work).
behavior: Every initiative MUST have a hypothesis, success_criteria, and completion_gate before it can move from lifecycle_state=proposed to approved. Proposed initiatives are never dispatched.

When focus_lock=true and lifecycle_state=approved|active: the expediter multiplies ROI of all lugs in this initiative by 3x. Non-initiative lugs are de-weighted to 0.5x. One focus lock allowed at a time.

Lifecycle: proposed -> [user approves hypothesis+criteria] -> approved -> [expediter routes epics] -> active -> [all epics complete] -> measuring -> [N nightly runs collect data, assessor scores against criteria] -> [user checkpoint] -> complete | modified

Measuring phase: after all epics in an initiative complete, lifecycle_state moves to measuring. A Vera scout (initiative-health-scout) queries the data_source, scores each criterion as passed/failed, and produces a hypothesis_result: confirmed|refuted|partial.
User checkpoints at two gates: (1) hypothesis approval (proposed->approved), (2) measurement review (measuring->complete). Changes to criteria after approval require a new user sign-off.
The wakeup brief surfaces the active focus-locked initiative prominently including: initiative label, lugs remaining, runs collected so far (measuring state), and hypothesis.

schema.hypothesis: string — the bet: if we build X, outcome Y will occur because Z
schema.success_criteria: array of strings — measurable, time-bound proof conditions

completion_gate.runs_required: int — nightly runs needed before measuring verdict
completion_gate.measurement_window_days: int — rolling window for data collection

schema.focus_lock: bool — expediter 3x ROI multiplier + 0.5x non-initiative lugs
schema.lifecycle_state: enum: proposed | approved | active | measuring | complete
schema.modification_notes: string — assessor recommendation after measuring | null

health.verification_note: focus-lock live in spoke_expediter.py (load_focus_lock_ids, 3x/0.5x multiplier). initiatives/index.json has all contract fields: lifecycle_state, focus_lock, hypothesis, success_criteria, completion_gate, approved_at, measuring_started_at, hypothesis_result. fleet-observability has focus_lock=true and lifecycle_state=active.

execute: 1. If any initiative is missing lifecycle_state or hypothesis: add the missing fields based on initiative description and current status. 2. If spoke_expediter.py lacks focus-lock logic: create an impl lug routed_to=BASHER targeting tools/spoke_expediter.py. 3. If measuring phase runner is missing: check score_backlog.py or relevant tool for measuring-phase integration. 4. Update health.last_impl_lug_completed to reflect most recently completed impl lug.
verify: All initiatives in index.json have lifecycle_state, focus_lock, hypothesis, success_criteria, completion_gate. tools/spoke_expediter.py has load_focus_lock_ids() returning correct IDs when focus_lock=true. Running spoke_expediter --dry-run shows Focus lock active when fleet-observability is locked.
